title: What grit for acrylic bathtub scratches?
problem: Acrylic bathtub scratches can look worse if sanding starts too coarse.
likely_cause: Acrylic is softer than ceramic or porcelain, so deep grit marks can be difficult to remove.
recommended_grit: Use 800 or 1000 for light scratches, then 1500, 2000, and 3000 before polishing.
steps: Polish with a plastic-safe polish after sanding.
avoid: Do not use coarse grit on a visible acrylic tub surface.
success_check: The scratch is reduced and the surface has an even fine haze before polishing.
quick_answer: For acrylic bathtub scratches, wet sand lightly with 800 or 1000, then 1500, 2000, and 3000 before polishing.
mistakes_to_avoid: Do not press hard enough to heat the acrylic.
Do not sand one spot too long.
search_phrases: what grit for acrylic bathtub scratches

title: What grit for fiberglass shower scratches?

avoid: Do not use coarse grit on a finished fiberglass shower surface.
success_check: The area has an even fine haze and no deep visible sanding lines.
quick_answer: For fiberglass shower scratches, wet sand with 800 or 1000, then 1500, 2000, and 3000 before polishing.
mistakes_to_avoid: Do not sand through the surface layer.
Do not polish while deep scratches remain.
search_phrases: what grit for fiberglass shower scratches

recommended_grit: Do not start with coarse grit. For minor test areas only, use 2000 or 3000 wet and polish afterward.

avoid: Do not use coarse sandpaper on a quartz countertop.

avoid: Do not use coarse grit on polished granite.
